step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion drawn previously. The rationale remains sound—the reduction in free-way space directly correlates with increased denture pressure which then leads to mucosal irritation through prolonged contact and friction. An additional point could be added regarding how this condition may affect patient comfort; patients often report difficulty eating and speaking comfortably if their dentures do not have adequate room, further exacerbating any existing irritations caused by excessive pressure. 

Answer Choice: A. Mucosal irritation.
